red_baseline.reason: T007 owns the failure-first operation-journal, crash, recovery, retention, index-rebuild, and no-replay contract; T008 owns its implementation.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED rerun: all three test files fail only because the T008-owned src/control-plane/operation-journal.js module is missing.

validation.summary: All three T007 fault-first test files parse successfully.
0 passed and 3 files failed at the missing T008-owned operation-journal module; the exact RED was rerun after the final test correction.
Syntax check passed for 63 JavaScript files.
No whitespace errors in T007 test and lifecycle paths.

review.evidence: The suite is test-only and freezes authoritative per-record state, derived indexes, atomic revision transitions, exact terminal/unresolved retention, fail-closed newer versions, deterministic crash windows, and no-replay recovery. It does not make the index authoritative, duplicate resource truth, or introduce an execution path.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Recovery tests permit only get/reconcile of the original record and prove the mutation handler/effect counters never increase during recovery.
Every post-dispatch crash window is represented by durable original evidence; no test authorizes CLI/MCP fallback or a second mutation.
The handler asserts the record is already durable and running before its first declared effect, while after-prepared and after-running faults retain zero effects.
Running and indeterminate recovery uses evidence-only reconciliation and handler/effect counters remain exactly one.
The tests explicitly model recoverable and indeterminate outcomes instead of claiming universal exactly-once execution.
Same ID/same digest returns or reconciles the original record; same ID/different digest refuses without changing it.
Prepared/running/indeterminate states never transition back into handler execution and unresolved evidence is retained without age expiry.
